data.supersedes_notes: split per R-005 1h rule → TASK-R1a + TASK-R1b

data.spec: Derive the category taxonomy with definitions + train.csv frequencies + value ranking (frequency × expected gain). DOC only, no classifier code.
data.title: Category taxonomy + value ranking (doc only)

data.spec: Implement classify(prompt)->category + offline tests; report classification coverage on train. Depends on R1a's taxonomy.
data.title: Rule classifier + tests for the taxonomy

data.spec: Port winner's train_sft.py to a clean training entrypoint; assert rank<=32/alpha 64 at runtime; --smoke path runs on a toy model (no GPU); writes adapter/ + cv_score.json. Includes offline test that rejects rank=64.
data.title: QLoRA train.py + --smoke offline test (rank<=32/alpha 64 asserted)

data.spec: kernel-metadata.json: GPU enabled, base model + dataset attached as placeholders; README documents the push->poll->pull workflow for the kaggle_gpu backend.
data.title: Kaggle kernel-metadata.json + README for the training kernel

data.spec: Skim all 217 discussion-*.md; produce a ranked 'do' list + 'avoid' list with discussion-id citations and short evidence per item.
data.title: 217 discussions → ranked Do / Avoid digest (cited)

data.spec: Using R11a + winner files, write a short deltas section and ≥5 concrete candidate hypotheses (statement, expected effect, how to test) ready for the operator to create_hypothesis.
data.title: Deltas vs winner + ≥5 new hypotheses for the ledger

data.spec: Read references/winner-lr_schedule.py.md, winner-loss_config.py.md, winner-train_common.py.md; produce ONE coherent digest covering LR schedule, loss/regularization choices, and the shared training utilities — each with file/line citations and how we replicate it in TASK-R10a.
data.title: Trio digest: winner LR schedule + loss config + train_common (clubbed)

data.spec:
Verify Jules has Kaggle access wired via the VM env vars. Run (capturing stdout):
  python tools/kaggle_lite.py whoami
  python tools/kaggle_lite.py submissions nvidia-nemotron-model-reasoning-challenge
  python tools/kaggle_lite.py leaderboard nvidia-nemotron-model-reasoning-challenge
Write the THREE results (status + first 30 lines of body each) into competitions/nvidia-nemotron-model-reasoning-challenge/references/kaggle-access-smoke.md. Then in the PR Summary state whether kaggle_user is correct, today/cap (5), and our current LB position. NEVER print or commit KAGGLE_API_TOKEN / KAGGLE_USERNAME values themselves.
data.title: Kaggle access smoke test from the Jules VM

data.supersedes_notes: legacy stub; replaced by R*a children (done) and per-category template R2.<cat>
legacy stub; replaced by R*a children (done) and per-category template R2.<cat>
legacy stub; replaced by R*a children (done) and per-category template R2.<cat>
legacy stub; replaced by R*a children (done) and per-category template R2.<cat>

data.expected_effect: +0.20 LB (matches winner 0.85 vs naive baselines ~0.65)
data.statement: DATA QUALITY is the dominant lever: per-category deterministic solvers + verified  oxed{} CoT corpora beat fancier training regimes.

data.expected_effect: matches winner; cheaper variants underperform
data.statement: LoRA rank 32 / alpha 64 SFT (winner config) is the right anchor; deviations should justify themselves with CV evidence.

data.expected_effect: simpler training; no degradation
data.statement: 1-epoch SFT is sufficient given high-quality CoT; more epochs over-fit on synthetic patterns.

data.expected_effect: saves compute; lower risk
data.statement: RL / distillation are NOT needed; pure SFT on verified CoT reaches top-LB.

data.expected_effect: +0.16 LB from solving all 1576 cipher rows in train + matching test distribution
data.statement: Cipher-category problems reduce to substitution+offset and admit a 100% deterministic solver.

data.expected_effect: +0.07 LB on equation_numeric_deduce(596)+_guess(136) categories
data.statement: Equation-numeric problems are solvable with SymPy linear/quadratic solvers; algebraic CoT is reproducible.

data.expected_effect: +0.01-0.03 LB vs random ordering, free improvement
data.statement: Difficulty-ordered curriculum SFT (easy→hard within each category) generalizes better than random shuffle on the hidden test set.

data.expected_effect: ~equal LB, 2x cheaper experiments
data.statement: LoRA rank 16 (half the cap) reaches within 0.005 of rank-32 with half the parameters; ablation worth running for compute efficiency.

data.expected_effect: +0.17 LB on bit_manipulation category
data.statement: Bit-manipulation problems use nickname encodings (e.g. "the integer formed by bytes…"); a robust regex extractor across all 1602 bit_manipulation rows yields 100% solver coverage.

data.spec: Implement data/solvers/cipher/solve.py (substitution+offset detection from prompt, exact answer) and generate.py (fresh cipher problems with known answers). Offline tests: report accuracy on 100 cipher rows from train; document any sub-types not yet handled. Goal: 100% solve rate on solvable instances.
data.title: Cipher-category deterministic solver + 100-sample holdout validation

data.spec: Implement data/solvers/equation_numeric/solve.py using SymPy: parse the equation system from prompt, solve(deduce: solve for the asked variable; guess: enumerate up to N candidates and verify). Offline tests on 50 deduce + 30 guess rows. Document failure modes.
data.title: Equation-numeric SymPy solver + verification on holdout

data.spec: Implement data/solvers/bit_manipulation/solve.py: detect bit nicknames (32/64-bit byte-form descriptions), reconstruct the integer, apply the requested operation, return exact answer. Test on 100 rows from train. Goal: 100% on solvable.
data.title: Bit-manipulation deterministic solver via nickname regex extraction

data.spec: Add data/curation/curriculum.py: per-row difficulty score (length, category fingerprint, solver-confidence), sort training data easy->hard, expose as a config option the training kernel can opt into. Offline tests on a small synthetic batch.
data.title: Difficulty estimator + curriculum-shuffle SFT config

data.spec: Clone kernels/train/ to kernels/train-rank16/, change config to LoRA rank=16/alpha=32, update kernel-metadata.json. Verify --smoke path runs; existing rank<=32 assertion still passes (16<=32).
data.title: Rank-16 LoRA ablation: kernel-metadata variant + smoke test

data.expected_effect: +0.17 LB from solving all 1597 gravity rows (16.8% of train)
data.statement: Gravity-category problems (free-fall/projectile/orbital mechanics) reduce to closed-form physics formulas and admit a deterministic solver.

data.expected_effect: +0.16 LB on 1576 numeral rows
data.statement: Numeral-system problems (base conversions, radix arithmetic, novel digit systems) are deterministic given the rules in the prompt.

data.expected_effect: +0.17 LB on 1594 unit_conversion rows
data.statement: Unit-conversion problems decompose into a directed graph of conversion factors; a Floyd-Warshall closure over the parsed factors yields exact answers.

data.expected_effect: +0.07 LB on 659+164 cryptarithm rows
data.statement: Cryptarithm-deduce problems are uniquely solvable letter-digit assignment problems; constraint propagation (or python-constraint / Z3) yields the exact answer.

data.spec: Implement data/solvers/gravity/solve.py: parse the physics prompt (find v0/g/h/angle/m/etc), apply the appropriate closed-form (kinematic eqs, projectile, orbital), return exact answer ( oxed{}). Also generate.py producing fresh gravity problems with known answers. Offline tests on 100 train rows from the gravity category. Target 100% on solvable.
data.title: Gravity-category deterministic solver (closed-form mechanics) + 100-sample validation

data.spec: Implement data/solvers/numeral/solve.py: parse digit alphabet + base from prompt, convert input numeral to/from decimal, perform asked arithmetic, return exact answer. generate.py for synthetic numeral problems. Offline tests on 100 train rows.
data.title: Numeral-system deterministic solver (arbitrary radix + novel digit systems)

data.spec: Implement data/solvers/unit_conversion/solve.py: parse the unit-to-unit conversion factors from the prompt into a graph, run Floyd-Warshall transitive closure for the asked source->target, apply to the asked value, return exact answer. generate.py with synthetic conversion problems. Tests on 100 train rows.
data.title: Unit-conversion deterministic solver via Floyd-Warshall conversion graph

data.spec: Implement data/solvers/cryptarithm/solve.py: parse letter equation from prompt, search letter-digit assignments via constraint propagation (use python-constraint if available, else hand-rolled backtracking; Z3 acceptable but heavier dep), return the unique assignment. Offline tests on 50 deduce + 30 guess rows. Document any pathological cases.
data.title: Cryptarithm-deduce solver via constraint propagation (python-constraint / Z3)

data.spec: Extend eval/cv.py (from R8): add per-category accuracy breakdown using the taxonomy classifier (from R1b once landed; until then use a simple keyword router as fallback). Stratified holdout split keeping category balance. Produce a Markdown report with per-category accuracies.
data.title: Extend CV harness to report per-category accuracy + stratified split

data.expected_effect: +0.01-0.03 LB direct from a category the winner never trained on.
data.statement: Winner skipped cryptarithm_guess; adding a solver + verified CoT for it gives direct LB headroom (164 train rows).

data.expected_effect: ~equal LB at half cost; may slightly improve generalization.
data.statement: Select2Reason-style curation (drop low-utility / repetitive traces) matches baseline accuracy at ~30 percent of cost.

data.expected_effect: +0.01 LB on equation_numeric category.
data.statement: Default to absolute-difference fallback in equation_numeric solvers when no rule is found; captures edge cases.

data.expected_effect: Unblocks training on free GPU. Critical precondition.
data.statement: Explicit Mamba mixer LoRA targeting (mixer.in_proj, mixer.out_proj) + attention-only avoids the MoE expert-scan hang and enables training on 2xT4 4-bit.

data.expected_effect: +0.01 LB on hard categories.
data.statement: Filtering out examples whose base-model min-logprob > -0.05 prevents over-tuning on easy examples and improves accuracy on hard rules.

data.notes: Smoke run (10 steps) on 2xT4 4-bit with explicit Mamba+attention targets. Validates no MoE-scan hang.
Assemble corpus.jsonl from 7 landed solvers + verified CoT; stratified train/dev; winner build_datum mask convention.
Winner-faithful baseline. rank=32, alpha=64, 1 epoch, LinearDecay 2e-5->1e-5, Mamba+attention targets. Goal ~0.85.
Full corpus -> top-utility filtered (~30 percent retained).
Add cryptarithm_guess CoT to corpus (winner skipped). Highest-confidence direct LB.
Combine winning ablation choices into final config. Train + submit (gated).

data.decision: Lock experimentation plan (Phase A done, B->C->D->E->F; E-007 cryptarithm_guess prioritized).
data.rationale: Cross-validated against winner repo (R13), discussion deltas (R11b), 18 ledger hypotheses. See EXPERIMENTATION_PLAN.md.

data.spec: Clone kernels/train/ to kernels/train-smoke-mamba/. Change LoRA target_modules to the EXPLICIT list ['mixer.in_proj','mixer.out_proj','q_proj','k_proj','v_proj','o_proj'] (NOT a regex). Set max_steps=10 (smoke only). Keep rank=32, alpha=64, 4-bit. Update kernel-metadata.json. The goal is to prove peft.get_peft_model() does NOT hang on the MoE expert scan on 2xT4. Offline test that asserts target_modules is a list of literal strings (no regex). Document the result expected: training loop starts within 30s of model load.
data.title: Smoke-train kernel (10 steps) validating Mamba mixer LoRA targeting

data.spec: Build data/corpus/v1/build_corpus.py that walks the 7+ landed solvers in data/solvers/<category>/, calls each to generate verified prompt+CoT+answer triples, writes corpus.jsonl in the winner's build_datum format (tokens, mask aligned per references/analysis-winner-training-internals.md): mask=1 ONLY on the CoT-reasoning + boxed-answer span, mask=0 on the prompt. Stratified 90/10 train/dev split keeping category frequencies. Emit corpus_index.jsonl listing per-row id/category/token_counts. Tests: format validation, mask correctness, stratification quality.
data.title: Assemble corpus.jsonl v1 from landed solvers (winner build_datum convention)

data.spec: Clone kernels/train/ to kernels/train-baseline-e002/. Final config: LoRA rank=32, alpha=64, 1 epoch, LinearDecayLRSchedule 2e-5 -> 1e-5 (per winner-lr_schedule.py), AdamW weight_decay=0.01, BF16 mixed precision, Mamba+attention target list from E-PRECOND, grad-accum for the largest stable micro-batch on 2xT4 4-bit, eval at end of epoch using eval/cv.py, write adapter/ + cv_score.json on success. Loss objective: maximize min log-prob over the masked CoT+answer span per winner-loss_config.py (CrossEntropyLossConfig baseline; no clipping/branch-weighting). Reject if smoke fails. Offline test that asserts rank<=32 and 1 epoch.
data.title: Finalize winner-faithful baseline training kernel (rank32/alpha64/1-epoch/LinearDecay)

data.spec: Sync kernels/train-rank16/ with E-002's config (LR schedule, target list, loss, data path) but with rank=16, alpha=32. Update kernel-metadata.json. Verify rank-16 via the existing assertion test (rejects rank 64 still passes). One change vs E-002.
data.title: Align rank-16 ablation kernel (R24) with the E-002 config except rank/alpha

data.spec: Clone E-002 kernel to kernels/train-2epoch-e004/. Single change: total_epochs=2. Keep LinearDecayLRSchedule's denominator updated accordingly. Test asserts epochs=2.
data.title: Clone baseline kernel with epochs=2 (refutation test for H-1EPOCH)

data.spec: Clone E-002 kernel to kernels/train-curriculum-e005/. Replace random shuffle of corpus.jsonl with the difficulty-ascending sort from data/curation/curriculum.py (R23). Offline test asserts the corpus order matches the curriculum config.
data.title: Wire R23 curriculum config into the baseline kernel (difficulty-ascending)

data.spec: Add data/curation/select2reason.py that scores each corpus row by utility (trace length / category novelty / dedup-fingerprint), keeps top ~30 percent. Then clone E-002 kernel to kernels/train-select2reason-e006/ pointing at the curated subset. Tests: dedup correctness; stratification preserved; subset size.
data.title: Select2Reason curation: keep top-utility ~30 percent of corpus, then train baseline

data.spec: CRITICAL PRIORITY: the winner skipped cryptarithm_guess (164 train rows). Use the cryptarithm solver from TASK-R28 to add verified CoT for cryptarithm_guess to the corpus (extend data/corpus/v1/build_corpus.py with the new category). Clone E-002 kernel to kernels/train-e007-cryptarithm/ pointing at the extended corpus. Tests: cryptarithm_guess rows present + verified.
data.title: Integrate cryptarithm_guess solver into corpus (winner skipped this category) — priority

data.spec: Add data/curation/logprob_filter.py: inference-pass the BASE model over corpus, record per-row min token log-prob, drop rows where min_logprob > -0.05. Clone E-002 kernel to kernels/train-logprob-filter-e008/ pointing at the filtered corpus. Document the dropped-rows histogram by category in the PR.
data.title: Pre-filter corpus by base-model min log-prob (drop already-easy examples)

data.spec: Operator runs this only after E-003..E-008 results are in. Build kernels/train-synthesis-e009/ combining the ablation choices whose CV beat E-002 (read state.json experiments collection for cv_score). Each combined choice cited in the kernel README. This is the final training run before finalize.
data.title: Synthesis: combine winning ablation choices into final config

data.expected_effect: Per technique-backlog #2 (effort:M, expected_gain:HIGH); enables every other iteration and prevents wasted submits against the 5/day hard cap; mirrors winner dashboards.
data.how_to_test: Run harness on the winner-faithful baseline adapter (from E-002 once it lands), record cv_score.json, confirm metric reproduces across 3 reruns within +/-0.5pp; then validate gate refuses to forward a deliberately-broken adapter to submit.
data.statement: Building eval/vllm_eval.py that loads (base + adapter), runs inference with temperature=0.0/top_p=1.0/max_tokens=7680/max_model_len=8192/max_lora_rank=32 and extracts \boxed{...} for exact (and +/-1e-2 numeric) match against a held-out CV slice gives us a reproducible local CV metric that the operator-side submit gate can compare against best_cv, preventing burned submit slots on duplicates and surfacing regressions before they hit the leaderboard.
data.title: Local vLLM CV/eval harness mirroring exact host inference params unlocks beats-best-CV submit gate

data.expected_effect: Saves >=1 wasted submit/day in practice (real cap is 5/day, 2 reserved finals); makes the unsupervised auto-merge safer because broken-adapter PRs cannot accidentally trigger a submit.
data.how_to_test: Inject a deliberately broken adapter (rank=64) and confirm gate fails; inject an adapter that outputs no \boxed{} and confirm gate fails; pass a valid baseline adapter and confirm gate returns 0.
data.statement: A tools/validate_adapter.py that (a) loads base+adapter via peft, (b) asserts the adapter_config rank <= 32, (c) runs 5 sample prompts through the adapter and confirms each output contains \boxed{...}, and (d) returns 0 only on full pass - used as a precondition by both the dispatcher submission_proposal flow and the operator submit gate - eliminates wasted submits on adapters that fail to load, exceed rank, or omit the required answer format.
data.title: Pre-submit adapter-validation gate (loadability + rank<=32 + boxed-output smoke) prevents wasting submits on broken adapters

data.acceptance_criteria: eval/vllm_eval.py exists; CLI works in mock mode (no vllm needed); cv_score.json schema includes aggregate + per_category + n_boxed_missing; pytest -q green.
data.definition_of_done: One PR; tests green; mock-mode roundtrip documented in PR body; hypothesis_id=H-VLLM-EVAL noted.
data.spec: Create eval/vllm_eval.py implementing: (1) CLI: --base /path/to/Nemotron-3-Nano-30B-A3B-BF16 --adapter <adapter_dir> --eval-jsonl <path> --out cv_score.json --max-samples N. (2) Inference config exactly matching host: temperature=0.0, top_p=1.0, max_tokens=7680, max_model_len=8192, max_lora_rank=32. (3) For each row: render the host-style chat template, get completion, extract last \boxed{...} via regex, normalize numeric answers (allow +/-1e-2 for numeric; exact-string otherwise - match host scoring). (4) Emit cv_score.json with aggregate, per_category, n_total, n_correct, n_boxed_missing. (5) NO real GPU run required in this task - gate the vllm import so the harness is unit-testable without vllm installed (mock mode flag). (6) Add tests/test_vllm_eval.py covering boxed extraction + per-category aggregation on a 5-row fixture. Acceptance: pytest -q passes; mock-mode CLI roundtrip on the 5-row sample produces a valid cv_score.json schema.
data.title: Write eval/vllm_eval.py harness skeleton with host-faithful params + \boxed{} extractor + per-category scoring

data.acceptance_criteria: CLI works in --mock mode; rank>32 -> nonzero CODE=RANK_TOO_HIGH; missing files -> nonzero CODE=MISSING_FILES; pytest -q green.
data.definition_of_done: One PR; tests green; PR body lists every exit CODE; hypothesis_id=H-SUBMIT-GATE noted.
data.spec: Create tools/validate_adapter.py with CLI: --adapter <adapter_dir> --base <base_path> [--prompts <path_to_5_smoke_prompts.jsonl>] [--mock]. Behavior: (1) Load adapter_config.json, assert r <= 32 (else exit nonzero with reason CODE=RANK_TOO_HIGH). (2) Verify required files exist (adapter_config.json + adapter_model.{safetensors,bin}); else CODE=MISSING_FILES. (3) Optionally: in non-mock mode, peft.PeftModel.from_pretrained(base, adapter) and run 5 smoke prompts at temperature=0.0; for each completion assert \boxed{...} regex present; else CODE=BOXED_MISSING (report which prompt). (4) Output validation_report.json {ok:bool, code:str|null, rank:int, n_smoke:int, n_boxed_ok:int}. (5) Add tests/test_validate_adapter.py covering: rank-too-high path (mock), missing-files path, mock-mode pass path. Acceptance: pytest -q passes; CLI returns 0 on a valid adapter dir (mock mode), nonzero on the failure cases.
data.title: Write tools/validate_adapter.py pre-submit gate (loadability + rank<=32 + boxed-output smoke)

data.expected_effect: Removes the only manual hand-off in the loop; enables the operator's 30-min cron to also drive Kaggle GPU runs autonomously; unlocks the full E-002 -> E-003..E-009 ablation fanout to run end-to-end.
data.how_to_test: After E-002 baseline kernel PR merges, run a single tick - confirm gpu_dispatch auto-fires, kernel-push returns a Kaggle kernel URL, kernel-status terminalizes, kernel-output pulls cv_score.json + adapter/ into experiments/<exp-id>/, and the operator's add_metric op records the new cv into best_cv if better.
data.statement: Today the loop stops at 'kernel authored by Jules and merged to main' - the actual Kaggle GPU run is a manual operator action. Adding a gpu_dispatch ledger op + a tools/run_kernel.py runner (kernel-push -> poll kernel-status -> kernel-output) lets the operator's owner-role automatically promote a merged experiment with status=READY_FOR_GPU into a live Kaggle kernel run and capture cv_score.json + adapter dir into the ledger, completing the train->validate loop.
data.title: Wiring gpu_dispatch into the loop closes the SDLC training cycle end-to-end (Jules authors kernel -> operator pushes -> kernel runs on 2xT4 -> outputs ingested as cv_score)

data.acceptance_criteria: CLI works in --mock mode end-to-end; exit codes correct on each branch; JSON summary contains kernel_url/terminal_state/cv_aggregate/elapsed_secs; pytest -q green.
data.definition_of_done: One PR; tests green; PR body shows --mock CLI output sample; hypothesis_id=H-GPU-DISPATCH.
data.spec: Create tools/run_kernel.py with CLI: --kernel-dir <path> --owner <kaggle_user> --slug <kernel-slug> --out-dir <experiments/<exp-id>/> --poll-secs 30 --timeout-mins 240 [--mock]. Behavior: (1) Call tools.kaggle_lite.kernel-push for the directory; capture returned ref. (2) Loop kaggle_lite.kernel-status every poll-secs until terminal (complete/error/cancelled) or timeout. (3) On complete: call kaggle_lite.kernel-output to download into out-dir. (4) Parse out-dir/cv_score.json if present; print a one-line summary {kernel_url, terminal_state, cv_aggregate, elapsed_secs}. (5) --mock short-circuits to a deterministic fake (returns terminal_state='complete', cv_aggregate=0.50 fixture). (6) Exit 0 on terminal-complete, 1 on terminal-error, 2 on timeout. Add tests/test_run_kernel.py covering --mock happy path + timeout simulation + JSON-output schema.
data.title: Write tools/run_kernel.py end-to-end runner (kernel-push -> poll status -> pull outputs)

data.acceptance_criteria: gpu_dispatch op accepted by state.py schema; loop.py invokes tools/run_kernel.py with --mock honored via env; gpu_runs records state transitions; pytest -q green.
data.definition_of_done: One PR; tests green; PR body shows ledger ops list updated; hypothesis_id=H-GPU-DISPATCH.
data.spec: Extend the orchestrator to dispatch merged kernel experiments to Kaggle GPU automatically. (1) state.py: add op 'gpu_dispatch' with data={experiment_id, kernel_dir, owner, slug, out_dir}; appends to state['gpu_runs'][slug] = {state:'QUEUED', experiment_id, started_at, kernel_url:None}. (2) loop.py.apply_decision: if decision contains gpu_dispatch ops, invoke tools.run_kernel.py (via subprocess.run with --mock=$RUN_KERNEL_MOCK) and update gpu_runs[slug] with terminal_state + cv_aggregate. (3) loop.py.poll_in_flight: extend to also poll non-terminal gpu_runs and update their state. (4) Add tests/test_gpu_dispatch.py exercising the mock path. Acceptance: an experiment with status=READY_FOR_GPU + kernel_dir set, when included in a decision's gpu_dispatch list, ends up with gpu_runs[slug].state in {COMPLETE,ERROR,TIMEOUT} and (on complete) cv_aggregate captured. pytest -q green.
data.title: Wire gpu_dispatch op into orchestrator/loop.py + state.py (auto-run merged kernels)

data.acceptance_criteria: Builder script works on a 50-row synthetic stub in the test; produces jsonl with category stratification within ±2 rows of target; pytest -q green; real fixture file is gitignored.
data.definition_of_done: One PR; tests green; README documents the 'why answers stay gitignored' rule; hypothesis_id=H-VLLM-EVAL.
data.spec: Create competitions/nvidia-nemotron-model-reasoning-challenge/eval/build_cv_fixture.py + produce eval/cv_fixture.jsonl. Behavior: (1) Loads train.csv from data/raw/ (fetched by tools/download_competition_data.py; if missing, the script must exit nonzero with a clear 'run download_competition_data.py first' message). (2) Classifies each row into one of the 9 host categories using the same classify.py logic that R28 merged (re-import; do NOT duplicate code). (3) Selects 200 rows stratified across categories (proportional or floor=15/cat — document choice). (4) Writes eval/cv_fixture.jsonl with {id, prompt, answer, category}. (5) The fixture itself is gitignored (it contains answers); only the builder script + a README explaining the build are committed. (6) Add tests/test_build_cv_fixture.py using a 50-row stub of train.csv (synthetic, no real data) to verify stratification + jsonl schema.
data.title: Build held-out CV fixture (200 rows stratified across 9 categories) for vllm_eval.py

data.acceptance_criteria: record_cv_score op accepted; best_cv auto-updates only when strictly better; tests cover both branches; pytest -q green.
data.definition_of_done: One PR; tests green; PR body shows status snapshot before/after mock; hypothesis_id=H-VLLM-EVAL.
data.spec: (1) orchestrator/state.py: add op 'record_cv_score' with data={experiment_id, cv_aggregate, per_category, n_total, n_correct, n_boxed_missing, source_kernel:str|None}. Appends to state['metrics'][f'cv:{experiment_id}'] = data; if cv_aggregate > state.get('best_cv', -inf), set state['best_cv'] = cv_aggregate AND state['best_cv_source'] = experiment_id. (2) orchestrator/loop.py.apply_decision: after a gpu_dispatch completes successfully, automatically read out_dir/cv_score.json and emit a record_cv_score op into the patch. (3) tools/status.py: update the snapshot to show best_cv + best_cv_source. (4) Add tests/test_record_cv_score.py covering: new best_cv -> updates; lower cv -> recorded but best_cv unchanged; per_category serialized. Acceptance: pytest -q green; status snapshot shows best_cv after a mock gpu_dispatch run.
data.title: Add record_cv_score state op + auto best_cv update + apply_decision pickup from kernel-output

data.acceptance_criteria: CLI builds a valid submission.zip from a stub adapter; rank>32 path errors; manifest.json included; pytest -q green.
data.definition_of_done: One PR; tests green; PR body shows zip listing; hypothesis_id=H-SUBMIT-GATE.
data.spec: Create tools/package_submission.py with CLI: --adapter <adapter_dir> --out <submission.zip> [--cv-score <path>] [--manifest <path>]. Behavior: (1) Validate adapter dir has adapter_config.json + adapter_model.{safetensors,bin}; validate rank <= 32 (same check as validate_adapter.py - import that function, don't duplicate). (2) Build submission.zip with the host-required layout (adapter_config.json at root, adapter_model.safetensors at root, tokenizer files passed through if present). (3) Write a manifest.json inside the zip containing {cv_score, adapter_sha256, packed_at, experiment_id_if_provided}. (4) Print zip path + bytes + cv_score on stdout as JSON. (5) Add tests/test_package_submission.py covering: valid adapter -> zip created; rank>32 -> nonzero; missing files -> nonzero; zip contents include manifest.json. Acceptance: pytest -q green; CLI roundtrip on a stub adapter dir.
data.title: Write tools/package_submission.py that builds submission.zip from an adapter dir (Kaggle format)

data.acceptance_criteria: decide_submit covers all 4 skip branches and 1 happy path; loop.py integration calls competition_submit only on happy path; pytest -q green.
data.definition_of_done: One PR; tests green; PR body lists all skip reasons; hypothesis_id=H-SUBMIT-GATE.
data.spec: Add orchestrator/submit_gate.py module with function decide_submit(state, kaggle_lite) -> {action:'submit'|'skip', reason:str, candidate:{experiment_id, blob_token, cv_score} | None}. Behavior: (1) Find candidate experiments with status=READY_FOR_SUBMIT + recorded cv_score + blob_token. (2) Skip unless cv_score > state['best_cv'] by at least a configurable margin (default 0.0). (3) Skip unless today's auto-submit count < MAX_AUTO_SUBMITS_PER_DAY (env, default 3). (4) Skip unless live kaggle_lite.submissions(comp) cap-remaining >= 1 + 2 (reserve 2 finals). (5) Wire orchestrator/loop.py.apply_decision: when decide_submit returns submit, call tools.kaggle_lite.competition_submit and emit record_submission op + bump submits_by_day. (6) Add tests/test_submit_gate.py covering each skip branch + the happy submit path (mock kaggle_lite). Acceptance: pytest -q green; happy path emits submit decision + records; all 4 skip branches return correct reason strings.
data.title: Add submit_decision step to orchestrator/loop.py (cv-beats-best + cap-headroom + ≤3/day gating)

data.expected_effect: Removes one human-in-the-loop step per failure. Observed rate: 1/11 dispatches in the RUN-20260530-124519 fanout. At scale (50-100 sessions/day expected near deadline), this saves ~5-10 manual rescues/day.
data.how_to_test: Mock a session-state response with state=COMPLETED + pr_url=None and an activities response containing a valid unidiff. Run poll_in_flight - verify (1) a rescue branch is created, (2) patch applies cleanly, (3) pytest is invoked, (4) merge to main happens via the same github_ops.merge_pr code path, (5) the session is cleared. Also test the failure case where pytest fails: rescue branch is left in place but main is NOT modified.
data.statement: Tick RUN-20260530-131519 surfaced a real failure mode: session 290296703496810863 emitted sessionCompleted with a complete unidiff in its artifact (tools/package_submission.py + tests/test_package_submission.py) but never opened a GitHub PR. The operator had to manually extract the patch, apply it to a rescue branch, run pytest, and merge to main. Adding an operator-side auto-rescue path inside orchestrator/loop.py.poll_in_flight - when a session returns state=COMPLETED with pr_url=None, fetch /sessions/<sid>/activities, find the last sessionCompleted artifact gitPatch, apply it to a fresh branch, run pytest -q, merge to main via the existing R-007 force-merge path - eliminates this whole manual step.
data.title: Auto-rescue Jules sessions that fire sessionCompleted with a full patch but no PR (eliminates manual operator merges)

data.acceptance_criteria: auto-rescue exercises all 5 branches in unit tests; happy path produces a merge commit in a test fixture repo; pytest -q green.
data.definition_of_done: One PR; tests green; PR body lists the 5 branches; hypothesis_id=H-AUTO-RESCUE.
data.spec: Extend orchestrator/loop.py.poll_in_flight: when a session response has state=COMPLETED AND pr_url is empty, before clearing the session: (1) GET https://jules.googleapis.com/v1alpha/sessions/<sid>/activities (use the existing JulesClient or jules_pool routing). (2) Find the last activity with sessionCompleted and a non-empty artifact.changeSet.gitPatch.unidiffPatch; if none found, mark the session FAILED and skip rescue. (3) Create a temp rescue branch off main: 'operator-rescue/<sid>-<short_ts>'. (4) Write the patch to a temp file and run `git apply --whitespace=nowarn <patchfile>`. If apply fails, mark FAILED and skip. (5) Run pytest -q with subprocess.run; capture rc. If rc != 0, leave the rescue branch and mark the session FAILED with a rescue_failed note. (6) On rc==0: commit the staged files with message 'rescue: <task_id> from session <sid>'; switch to main; merge --no-ff with the same -X theirs strategy used by github_ops.merge_pr; scan diff for secrets (same regex KGAT_/AQ.Ab/sk-ant-); on secret leak, abort and mark FAILED. (7) On success: mark the task DONE and clear the session - emit the ops via the existing StatePatch flow, not a side effect. Add tests/test_auto_rescue.py covering: happy path (rescue + merge + DONE), pytest-fails path (rescue branch present, main untouched, FAILED), patch-fails path (FAILED, no branch), no-artifact path (FAILED, no branch), secret-leak path (FAILED, no merge). Acceptance: pytest -q green; happy path produces a real merge commit in a test git repo.
data.title: Add auto-rescue path to poll_in_flight (COMPLETED-without-PR -> extract patch + pytest + merge)

data.expected_effect: Catches a class of silent failures that would only manifest as 'CV looks 0.0 on a well-trained adapter' - very hard to debug after the fact.
data.how_to_test: Create a 10-prompt fixture with known correct host rendering (captured from Nemotron's HF tokenizer with default chat_template + reasoning_on system message). Run vllm_eval.py in render-only mode against the same fixture - assert byte-equality of every rendered prompt. Test fails if our template diverges.
data.statement: Discussions repeatedly note that the host scoring runs inference with a SPECIFIC chat template (Nemotron-3-Nano-30B-A3B-BF16 default + reasoning_on header). If our LoRA adapter is trained or evaluated under a different template (e.g. our eval/vllm_eval.py uses ChatML where host uses Nemotron's native), every output is a few tokens off and \boxed{} extraction may miss. Adding eval/test_chat_template_parity.py that loads the official tokenizer's apply_chat_template on a known fixture and asserts byte-equality vs the harness's rendered prompt prevents this.
data.title: Chat-template parity test against the host inference prompt eliminates silent-failure submissions

data.acceptance_criteria: Fixture has 10 rows across >=3 categories; test exercises byte-equality; pytest -q green (xfail allowed if harness uses hard-coded template).
data.definition_of_done: One PR; tests green; PR body documents whether harness already loads the official template; hypothesis_id=H-CHAT-TEMPLATE.
data.spec: (1) Create eval/chat_template_fixture.jsonl with 10 host-realistic prompts (mix of equation_numeric, cryptarithm, cipher, bit_manipulation - sample from existing data/curation classifier output, NOT raw train.csv since that is gitignored). (2) For each fixture row, write a 'expected_rendered' field containing the EXACT string the official Nemotron tokenizer's apply_chat_template returns for a {role=user, content=<prompt>} message with the reasoning_on system header matching the host. (Capture these offline using the HF tokenizer.) (3) Write tests/test_chat_template_parity.py that: imports the prompt-rendering function from competitions/.../eval/vllm_eval.py (which TASK-EVAL-vllm-harness-skeleton already provides) and asserts the rendered output byte-equals expected_rendered for all 10 rows. (4) If our vllm_eval.py uses a hard-coded template (current state), this task documents the gap and the test should be marked xfail with a TODO note pointing at a follow-up task to load the official tokenizer template. If our harness already loads the official template, the test should pass. Acceptance: pytest -q passes (including xfail if applicable); fixture jsonl committed with prompts but the answer column dropped (DATA.md rule).
data.title: Add eval/chat_template_fixture.jsonl + tests/test_chat_template_parity.py asserting byte-equality vs official tokenizer

data.acceptance_criteria: All 8 files have non-placeholder values; dry-run passes.
data.spec: Each kernel-metadata.json has dataset_sources=['TODO/REPLACE-WITH-CORPUS-DATASET-SLUG'] and model_sources=['TODO/REPLACE-WITH-REAL-MODEL-VERSION-FROM-KAGGLE']. Replace with the real Kaggle dataset slug owned by sai1881 holding corpus.jsonl v1 (built by TASK-E001), and the real model-version slug for Nemotron-3-Nano-30B-A3B-BF16. Validate with kaggle_lite kernel-push dry-run. Blocked on Q-018/Q-019.
data.title: Replace TODO dataset_sources + model_sources in 8 kernel-metadata.json files

data.acceptance_criteria: Kernel completes; outputs pulled; runtime <10 min; metrics logged.
data.definition_of_done: Result under experiments/SMOKE-001/; cv recorded via update_gpu_run.
data.spec: Push smoke-mamba kernel with reduced hyperparams (rank=4, max_steps=20, 100 rows). Validate full chain: kernel-push -> status=complete -> output-pull -> cv_score.json + adapter_config.json present. Do NOT submit. Steelman counter-arg 8.
data.title: Kaggle GPU smoke run: 5-min rank=4 max_steps=20 100-row preflight

data.acceptance_criteria: state.gpu_runs entry for the slug; cv_score numeric; rank<=32.
data.spec: Once smoke run succeeds: emit gpu_dispatch for experiment_id=E-002-baseline-rank32, backend=kaggle_gpu, slug=sai1881/train-baseline-e002. Poll until terminal; pull adapter/ + cv_score.json into experiments/E-002-baseline-rank32/; record cv; set experiments[E-002].cv_score + status=COMPLETED.
data.title: Push baseline E-002 (rank=32) kernel to Kaggle GPU and ingest cv_score

data.acceptance_criteria: Session terminal (COMPLETED + PR or canceled); lock released.
data.definition_of_done: No stale lock; ledger reflects terminal state.
data.spec: Session 13107147469450254389 stuck in out-of-scope edit retry loop (~35min idle, 26 identical refused patches). See F-057. Decision: (a) sendmessage steer to ship kernel-only + NEEDS_SPLIT, or (b) kill + re-dispatch with widened area. User decision needed.
data.title: Rescope or abort stuck E-007 cryptarithm-guess Jules session

data.acceptance_criteria: Scheduled trigger exists OR explicit manual-tick acceptance recorded.
data.definition_of_done: CronList/Task Scheduler shows entry; or feedback memory records decision.
data.spec: No cron exists (F-054). Pick: (a) /schedule @hourly running tools dispatch+status, (b) Windows Task Scheduler + claude -p (CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN), (c) accept manual-tick mode. User decision.
data.title: Schedule the autonomous operator tick (cron / Task Scheduler) - user approval

data.decision: Pause new feature work; land the 6 audit-corrections + 5 seeded tasks before adding capability.
data.rationale: Audit found 5 live defects (3 fixed mid-pass), 6 invalidated assumptions, 0 end-to-end runs. Infrastructure-complete but execution-untriggered. Adding features now compounds cross-PR invariant drift the audit identified.

data.detail:
merge conflict/failure: error: Your local changes to the following files would be overwritten by merge:
	competitions/nvidia-nemotron-model-reasoning-challenge/kernels/train-baseline-e002/train.py
Ple

data.note: v3 demo-code crashed on T4 OOM; v4 has 4-bit NF4 fallback

data.expected_effect: Decouples campaign throughput from the GPU bottleneck. Each research task produces a concrete file that influences the next code push. Most impactful: the CoT corpus (winner's 0.65->0.85 lever).
data.how_to_test: All 4 tasks ship files within ~2h; next code push uses findings from at least 1 of them.
data.statement: While the GPU iteration is single-threaded (we wait on browser Save&Run All), 4 parallel Jules sessions can mine top notebooks, dig through 200+ discussions, synthesize CoT corpus, and gap-analyze vs winner — all CPU-only, all producing concrete analysis/data files the operator reads on next cycle to inform the next code push and the next training experiment.
data.title: Parallel CPU-only research while GPU is blocked accelerates the loop without using submit-cap

data.spec: Read the top 5 most-upvoted public notebooks on the NVIDIA Nemotron Model Reasoning Challenge (use tools/kaggle_lite.py search_notebooks or competition page). For each notebook extract: (a) data prep + corpus path, (b) model load (4-bit vs bf16, device_map, max_memory), (c) LoRA config (rank/alpha/target_modules/dropout), (d) training loop (epochs, batch, accumulation, lr schedule, mask strategy), (e) inference template (chat format, max_new_tokens, temperature, boxed extraction), (f) special tricks (curriculum, log-prob filter, select2reason, etc.). Top candidates: ryanholbrook/nvidia-nemotron-submission-demo, asalhi/tinker-adapter-to-ready-to-submit-adapter, dennisfong/nvidia-nemotron-sfttrainer-training, kienngx/nvidia-nemotron-trained-models-submission, kienngx/nvidia-nemotron-training-cot-labels. Output: competitions/nvidia-nemotron-model-reasoning-challenge/analysis/top_notebooks_v1.md with a markdown table comparing all 5 + a ranked list of the top 5 techniques our current notebook_fork_working.ipynb is MISSING. Cite each technique with the notebook ref + line. Acceptance: file exists with >=5 notebooks compared + >=5 missing techniques identified.
data.title: Mine top 5 public NVIDIA Nemotron notebooks for techniques + write side-by-side comparison

data.spec: Read all discussion threads under competitions/nvidia-nemotron-model-reasoning-challenge/references/ (files discussion-*.md). Extract every actionable technique, gotcha, eval tip, GPU/memory trick, and submission-format insight. Categorize into: TRAIN_TRICK / DATA_TRICK / EVAL_TRICK / GPU_TIP / GOTCHA / SUBMIT_TIP. Rank by likely-impact-on-our-LB. Output: competitions/nvidia-nemotron-model-reasoning-challenge/analysis/discussions_insights_v1.md with: (a) top 20 insights ranked by expected delta, each citing source discussion file, (b) a 'fixes for our current notebook' section listing the 5 most-applicable ones with the exact code change to make. Acceptance: >=20 ranked insights + >=5 concrete code fixes citing line numbers in notebook_fork_working.ipynb.
data.title: Mine 200+ discussions for ranked actionable insights

data.spec: This is technique-backlog #1: the largest single CV-lever. Read references/analysis-winner.md + winner-reasoning.py.md + winner-corpus.py.md + winner-augmentation.py.md. Build competitions/nvidia-nemotron-model-reasoning-challenge/data/corpus/cot_v1.jsonl by: (a) loading data/raw/train.csv (run tools/download_competition_data.py if missing), (b) classifying each row into one of the 9 host categories using data/curation/classify.py, (c) for each row generating a step-by-step \boxed{} chain-of-thought using a per-category deterministic solver (Python, NO LLM/API calls). Cover at least 6 of the 9 categories (equation_numeric, cryptarithm, cipher, bit_manipulation, numeral, gravity priorities). Output schema per line: {id, category, prompt, cot_completion, verified:bool}. Target: >=500 verified-CoT rows. Add tests/test_cot_corpus_v1.py asserting each output row has \boxed{answer} matching the original train.csv answer (verification gate). Acceptance: corpus.jsonl exists with >=500 rows >=90 percent verified across >=6 categories; pytest -q green.
data.title: Build CoT corpus from train.csv using winner's deterministic solver pattern (the 0.65->0.85 lever)

data.spec: Compare the winner's solution (per references/analysis-winner.md + analysis-winner-training-internals.md + all winner-*.md files) to our current notebook_fork_working.ipynb. Produce competitions/nvidia-nemotron-model-reasoning-challenge/analysis/winner_vs_current_v1.md with: (a) side-by-side table of every winner design decision vs ours (data, LoRA, optimizer, training loop, inference, eval, submission format), (b) top 10 ranked GAPS where we differ from winner, each with expected CV delta if closed and the exact code change for our notebook, (c) recommended next 3 experiments in priority order. Acceptance: >=10 gaps listed with expected deltas + code changes; explicit top-3 experiment recommendation with hypothesis.
data.title: Gap analysis: winner solution vs our current fork notebook

data.expected_effect: Decision quality. Replaces 'operator skim 1MB of markdown' with 'operator reads 5-sheet xlsx with ranked experiments + code-change pseudocode for each.'
data.how_to_test: 4 workbooks materialize within ~6h Jules-wall-clock; next code push cites a specific xlsx row.
data.statement: Per-source markdown is hard to compare/rank. Structured Excel sheets with the same column vocabulary across sources (winner code, 217 discussions, community, tabs) make the operator's next-experiment decision provable: rank by (expected_cv_delta / effort), pick the top, push it.
data.title: Structured xlsx synthesis of every scraped source produces the highest-quality input for next code push

data.spec: Read ALL 17 files matching competitions/nvidia-nemotron-model-reasoning-challenge/references/winner-*.md (135KB total: train_sft.py, reasoning.py, corpus.py, loss_config.py, lr_schedule.py, augmentation.py, generate_csv.py, train_common.py, upload_adapter.py, notebook_tinker.py, serve.sh, README.md, delete-tinker-checkpoint.sh, .mcp.json, CLAUDE.md, pyproject.toml, INDEX.md). Build ONE Excel workbook at competitions/nvidia-nemotron-model-reasoning-challenge/analysis/winner_code_analysis.xlsx with these sheets: (1) 'Overview' — one row per file: filename | role | LoC | key_functions | depends_on | criticality_1to5. (2) 'TechnicalDetails' — one row per significant function/class across all 17 files: file | symbol | purpose | input | output | algorithm_summary | hyperparams_used | gotchas. (3) 'KeyImprovements' — one row per technique we should adopt: technique | source_file | source_lines | current_state_in_our_notebook | expected_cv_delta | priority_1to5 | code_change_pseudocode. (4) 'CoreApproach' — narrative cells: 'data_pipeline', 'training_recipe', 'inference_pipeline', 'evaluation_method', 'submission_packaging' — what the winner does end-to-end. (5) 'OpenQuestions' — things the code reveals about the winner's choices we don't yet understand. Use openpyxl. NO LLM/external API calls — pure file reading + parsing. Acceptance: xlsx exists with all 5 sheets populated; >= 17 rows on Overview; >= 30 rows on TechnicalDetails; >= 10 rows on KeyImprovements ranked by priority.
data.title: Excel: per-file deep analysis of all 17 winner-* code files

data.spec: Read ALL 217 files matching competitions/nvidia-nemotron-model-reasoning-challenge/references/discussion-*.md (1.08MB total). For each thread, extract structured rows. Build ONE Excel workbook at competitions/nvidia-nemotron-model-reasoning-challenge/analysis/discussions_extract.xlsx with these sheets: (1) 'Threads' — one row per thread: file | topic_id | title_inferred | category (TRAIN_TRICK / DATA_TRICK / EVAL_TRICK / GPU_TIP / GOTCHA / SUBMIT_TIP / META / RULES) | word_count | author_count_inferred | confidence_rating_1to5. (2) 'Insights' — one row PER extracted insight (multiple per thread allowed): thread_file | insight_text | category | applicability_to_our_notebook (YES/MAYBE/NO) | expected_cv_delta_qualitative (HIGH/MED/LOW) | concrete_code_change_if_applicable. (3) 'Top20Actionable' — the 20 highest-impact insights ranked by expected_cv_delta and applicability, with concrete code-change pseudocode for our notebook_fork_working.ipynb. (4) 'KnownGotchas' — every documented failure mode (P100, mamba-ssm versions, OOM, chat template, boxed extraction, submission format etc.) with the fix. Use openpyxl. Acceptance: xlsx with all 4 sheets; >=217 rows on Threads; >=100 rows on Insights; exactly 20 on Top20Actionable with code-change pseudocode.
data.title: Excel: structured extraction from all 217 discussion-*.md threads

data.spec: Read these reference files: (a) competitions/.../references/community-sources.md (95KB, multi-section), (b) all 6 analysis-*.md files, (c) all 7 tab-*.md files (overview, rules, data, code, models, leaderboard, discussion-list), (d) technique-backlog.md + DIGEST-community.md + scraped-index.md. Build ONE Excel workbook at competitions/nvidia-nemotron-model-reasoning-challenge/analysis/community_and_tabs.xlsx with these sheets: (1) 'CommunityCode' — every code-snippet/repo/notebook referenced in community-sources.md: source_block | author | what_it_does | key_techniques | applicable_to_us. (2) 'CompetitionFacts' — extracted from tab-overview/rules/data: fact | source_tab | implication_for_us (eval metric, daily cap, accelerator grant, scoring formula etc.). (3) 'TechniqueBacklog' — every ranked technique from technique-backlog.md + DIGEST-community.md: rank | technique | source | effort_S/M/L | expected_gain | rationale | status (TODO/DONE/SUPERSEDED). (4) 'OurAnalysisSummary' — distill our own 6 analysis-*.md files into key conclusions per file. Use openpyxl. Acceptance: xlsx with all 4 sheets; >=30 community-code rows; >=20 facts; >=9 backlog rows.
data.title: Excel: community-sources + 6 analyses + 7 tab pages + technique-backlog into one workbook

data.spec: This task RUNS AFTER the other 3 xlsx tasks land. Read the 3 workbooks: competitions/nvidia-nemotron-model-reasoning-challenge/analysis/winner_code_analysis.xlsx, competitions/nvidia-nemotron-model-reasoning-challenge/analysis/discussions_extract.xlsx, competitions/nvidia-nemotron-model-reasoning-challenge/analysis/community_and_tabs.xlsx. Synthesize them into ONE master workbook at competitions/nvidia-nemotron-model-reasoning-challenge/analysis/master_roadmap.xlsx with these sheets: (1) 'RankedExperiments' — every experiment we should run, ranked by (expected_cv_delta / effort): rank | name | hypothesis | source_evidence | expected_delta | effort_hours | depends_on | code_change_summary. (2) 'NextWeekPlan' — exactly which experiments to run in days 1-7 toward 0.85 LB; include a Gantt-style sheet: day | experiment | who (operator / Jules / GPU) | expected_outcome. (3) 'CurrentNotebookGaps' — line-by-line diff: what notebook_fork_working.ipynb does vs what winner does, with the exact change. (4) 'SubmissionStrategy' — when to submit, what cv-thresholds to gate on, how to use the 5/day cap intelligently across 15 days. Use openpyxl, read the input xlsx with openpyxl.load_workbook. BLOCKED until the other 3 xlsx tasks are DONE. Acceptance: xlsx with all 4 sheets; >=15 ranked experiments; 7-day plan; >=10 gap rows.
data.title: Excel: master roadmap consolidating findings from the 3 other workbooks into a single shippable plan

data.fix: chmod +x ptxas-blackwell from utility-script (Blackwell triton wheel ships non-executable on RTX Pro 6000)

data.expected_effect: From 'pipeline-validation' state to 'final-quality-runnable' state within 24h.
data.how_to_test: All 4 Jules ship within ~3h Jules wall-clock; v23 produces first real CV on next browser run; ablation_configs.py exposes 6 dicts.
data.statement: 4 Jules workers (corpus-scale 9cat, cv-fixture 9cat, host-template-parity, ablation-configs) + operator-side notebook v23 (chat template + reasoning_on + full corpus) + corpus-uploader tool close all 10 gaps from the readiness audit in parallel; once landed, the operator can run baseline + 5 ablations within 3 days.
data.title: Parallel close of all 10 gaps unblocks final-quality training within 24h

data.spec: Read existing data/corpus/v2/corpus.jsonl (398 rows, 4 cats) and references/analysis-winner.md + winner-reasoning.py.md + winner-augmentation.py.md + winner-corpus.py.md. Build competitions/nvidia-nemotron-model-reasoning-challenge/data/corpus/v4/corpus.jsonl with: (a) 9 categories covered: bit_manipulation, cipher, gravity, numeral, cryptarithm, cryptarithm_guess, equation_numeric, unit_conversion, select2reason. (b) >= 300 rows per category (3000+ total). (c) Use per-category deterministic Python solvers (NO LLM/API), mirror winner's reasoning.py logic. (d) Apply the 5 winner augmenters (paraphrase, perturbation, etc) per augmentation.py. (e) HARD verification: each row's completion must end with \boxed{X} where X exactly equals or numerically matches the original answer; reject mismatched rows; do NOT mark verified=True falsely. (f) Write data/corpus/v4/quality_report.json with per-category {generated, verified, rejected, samples}. (g) Add tests/test_corpus_v4_quality.py asserting >= 3000 rows, >= 95% verified, all 9 cats present. Acceptance: pytest -q green; quality report shows >= 95% verified for every category.
data.title: Scale CoT corpus to 3K+ rows across ALL 9 host categories (winner-grade volume)

data.spec: Read data/raw/train.csv (run tools/download_competition_data.py if missing) and data/curation/classify.py (the 9-category classifier from R28). Build competitions/nvidia-nemotron-model-reasoning-challenge/eval/cv_fixture_v2.jsonl with: (a) 300 rows total, stratified across 9 categories with floor(33/cat). (b) Schema per line: {id, category, prompt, answer}. (c) HARD requirement: rows MUST NOT appear in data/corpus/v4/corpus.jsonl (no train/eval leakage). (d) The fixture file itself is gitignored (contains answers); commit the builder script + README +   per-category count manifest at eval/cv_fixture_v2_manifest.json. (e) Add eval/build_cv_fixture_v2.py + tests/test_cv_fixture_v2.py asserting 300 rows, 9 cats, no overlap with corpus, schema valid. Acceptance: pytest -q green; manifest committed with counts.
data.title: Build held-out CV fixture (300 rows stratified across all 9 host categories)

data.spec: Per references/analysis-winner-training-internals.md + the winner repo's notebook_tinker.py, the host uses a SPECIFIC chat template + reasoning_on system header. Our v23 notebook now calls tokenizer.apply_chat_template — but we need parity tests. Build: (a) competitions/nvidia-nemotron-model-reasoning-challenge/eval/host_template_spec.md documenting the exact template, system prompt text,   max_new_tokens, temperature, top_p, max_model_len that the host uses (extract from analyses +   any official references). (b) competitions/nvidia-nemotron-model-reasoning-challenge/eval/test_template_parity.py: load tokenizer from a local fake or skip if not   available; render 5 fixed prompts via apply_chat_template + reasoning_on;   assert byte-equality vs expected strings in host_template_spec.md. Acceptance: spec.md exists with concrete strings; parity test runs (xfail allowed if local tokenizer isn't accessible). Cite source line numbers in analyses + winner repo.
data.title: Capture exact Nemotron host chat template + reasoning_on system prompt for parity testing

data.spec: Create a single Python module that yields 5 ablation configs as dicts (one per knob), each one compatible with our notebook's tunables (LORA_RANK, MAX_TRAIN, REASONING_ON, EPOCHS, etc). Place at competitions/nvidia-nemotron-model-reasoning-challenge/experiments/ablation_configs.py exposing CONFIGS = {'baseline':..., 'rank16':..., '2epoch':..., 'curriculum':..., 'select2reason':..., 'logprob_filter':...}}. For each, include: (a) the parameter delta from baseline (e.g. rank=16 instead of 32), (b) one-line hypothesis, (c) source citation (winner repo file:line or discussion id). Also write experiments/README.md explaining how the operator applies one config to the notebook via save_notebook (search/replace the tunable block). Add tests/test_ablation_configs.py asserting all 6 keys exist and each has the required fields. Acceptance: 6 configs total (baseline + 5); each has rank, alpha, epochs, lr, target_modules, hypothesis, source; pytest -q green.
data.title: Pre-build 5 ablation config JSONs (rank16, 2epoch, curriculum, select2reason, logprob_filter)

data.spec: Per analysis/master_roadmap.xlsx CurrentNotebookGaps + winner_code_analysis.xlsx KeyImprovements: our notebook trains on all tokens (unmasked) and uses epoch-based LR decay. Winner uses (a) target masking — loss only on CoT + answer span, not the user prompt; (b) STEP-based LinearDecayLRSchedule decaying per step within an epoch. Add to competitions/nvidia-nemotron-model-reasoning-challenge/experiments/notebook_patches/v24_target_masking.py a Python script that, when given the current notebook source as text input, returns an updated source with: (1) the datum() function correctly masks prompt tokens to 0, completion to 1 (currently broken when using chat templates — the prompt-only render may have different offsets); (2) the LR schedule decays per STEP not per epoch: lr = lr_initial + (lr_final - lr_initial) * (step / total_steps); (3) preserves the chat-template rendering already in v23. Add tests/test_notebook_patch_v24.py asserting that applying the patch to a stub notebook produces the expected diff (no exceptions, mask is 0 for system+user tokens, 1 for assistant).
data.title: Add target-masking + step-based LR decay tunable block to notebook (winner pattern)

data.spec: Per master_roadmap CurrentNotebookGaps + winner_code_analysis.xlsx: winner uses stratified batching across 9 categories — every gradient-accumulation window sees a mix of categories rather than one category in a contiguous block. Build competitions/nvidia-nemotron-model-reasoning-challenge/experiments/notebook_patches/v25_stratified.py (another patch script) that replaces the simple `for d in train_data` loop with: (1) groups train_data by row.category; (2) round-robin one row per category per micro-batch within a GA window; (3) shuffles categories at epoch start with a fixed seed. Falls back to plain iteration when 'category' is missing. Add tests/test_stratified_batching.py asserting category-balance within each GA window on a 9-cat stub. Acceptance: patch script exists; tests green.
data.title: Add stratified-batching sampler tunable block to notebook (winner pattern, per-category mix)
